summary.para: Represents an object which can be passed to an instance of ProjectAuditor to specify how analysis should be performed and to provide delegates to be called when analysis steps have completed. AnalysisParams defaults to values which instruct ProjectAuditor to analyze everything in the project for the current build target, but instances can be populated with custom data in an object initializer to provide additional constraints.
Assemblies to analyze. If null, all compiled assemblies will be analyzed.
Issue Categories to include in the audit. If null, the analysis will include all categories.
Code optimization mode. The default is CodeOptimization.Release.
The Compilation mode to use during code analysis. The default is CompilationMode.Player.

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.AnalysisParams.#ctor(System.Boolean)

summary.para: AnalysisParams constructor.

member.param: If true, the global ProjectSettings will register DiagnosticParams defaults, save any changes and copy the data into this object. This is usually the desired behaviour, but is not allowed during serialization.

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.AnalysisParams.#ctor(Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.AnalysisParams)

summary.para: Copy constructor.

member.param: The AnalysisParams object to copy from.

summary.para: The DiagnosticParams object which defines the customizable thresholds for reporting certain diagnostics. By default, this makes a copy of ProjectAuditorSettings.
Notifies that all Modules completed their analysis.
Reports a batch of new issues. Note that this be called multiple times per analysis.
Notifies that a Module completed its analysis.
Analysis platform. The default platform is the currently active build target.

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.AnalysisParams.WithAdditionalDiagnosticRules(System.Collections.Generic.List`1<Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.Rule>)

summary.para: Adds a list of additional Rules which will be applied during analysis.

member.param: Additional Rules to impose.

returns.para: This AnalysisParams object, after adding the additional Rules.

summary.para: Identifiers for the results of analysis for a Module and for a whole Report
Analysis was cancelled by the user
Analysis failed
Analysis is still in progress
Analysis completed successfully
Which area(s) of a project may be affected by a ReportItem.
Bitmask value representing all areas
Application size
Build time
CPU Performance
GPU Performance
Issues which affect iteration time in the Editor and can hamper productivity during development
Load times
Memory consumption
Indicates some error with the Descriptor data: A descriptor should never match no areas.
Quality. For example, using deprecated APIs that might be removed in the future
Required by platform. Typically this issue must be fixed before submitting to the platform store
Lack of platform support. For example, using APIs that are not supported on a specific platform and might fail at runtime
Options for selecting the code optimization level to be used during code analysis.
Debug code optimization
Release code optimization
Options for the compilation mode Project Auditor should use when performing code analysis.
Development player
Editor assemblies
Editor assemblies for Play Mode
Non-Development player (default)
Base class for a context object passed by a Module to an Analyzer's Analyze() method.

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.Core.AnalysisContext.CreateInsight(Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.IssueCategory,System.String)

summary.para: Create a ReportItemBuilder for an Insight: A ReportItem collected for informational purposes.

member.param: Issue category
User-friendly description

returns.para: The ReportItemBuilder, constructed with the specified category and description string

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.Core.AnalysisContext.CreateIssue(Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.IssueCategory,System.String,System.Object[])

summary.para: Create a ReportItemBuilder for an Issue: a potential problem in the project, with an actionable recommendation to resolve it.

member.param: Issue category
Descriptor ID
Arguments to be used in the message formatting

returns.para: The ReportItemBuilder, constructed with the specified category, descriptor ID and message arguments

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.Core.AnalysisContext.IsDescriptorEnabled(Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.Descriptor)

summary.para: Checks whether a given Descriptor is enabled for the current analysis.

member.param: The descriptor to check

returns.para: True if the Descriptor is applicable to the current target platform and Unity version, and if it's either enabled by default or by a Rule specified in Params. Otherwise, returns false.

summary.para: The AnalysisParams object that was passed to (or created by) the ProjectAuditor's Audit() method.
Abstract base class for an Analyzer to be invoked by AnimationModule
A context object passed by AssetsModule to an AssetsModuleAnalyzer's Analyze() method.
A path to an asset, of any type, in the project.
Abstract base class for an Analyzer to be invoked by AssetsModule

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.Core.AssetsModuleAnalyzer.Analyze(Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.Core.AssetAnalysisContext)

summary.para: Implement this method to detect Issues, construct ReportItem objects to describe them, and return them.

member.param: Context object containing information necessary to perform analysis

returns.para: An enumerator for a collection of ReportItem objects

summary.para: A context object passed by AudioClipModule to an AudioClipModuleAnalyzer's Analyze() method.
The AudioClip to be analyzed.
The file size of the imported AudioClip.
The AudioImporter used to import the AudioClip to be analyzed.
The name of an AudioClip in the project.
An estimate of the runtime memory footprint of this AudioClip, when it's playing.
The AudioImporter's sample settings.
Abstract base class for an Analyzer to be invoked by AudioClipModule

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.Core.AudioClipModuleAnalyzer.Analyze(Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.Core.AudioClipAnalysisContext)

summary.para: Implement this method to detect Issues, construct ReportItem objects to describe them, and return them.

member.param: Context object containing information necessary to perform analysis

returns.para: An enumerator for a collection of ReportItem objects

summary.para: Used to mark an integer field in an class that inherits from ModuleAnalyzer as being a Diagnostic Parameter.

member.param: The Diagnotic Parameter's name

The Diagnotic Parameter's tooltip text for project settings.

summary.para: The default value for this parameter.
The maximum value for this parameter.
The minimum value for this parameter.
The Diagnostic Parameter's name. This name should uniquely identify this parameter within a project.
Text about this DiagnosticParameter to show as a tooltip in Project Settings.
The Diagnostic Parameter's user friendly name as will be seen in Project Settings.

The material asset to be analyzed.
The name of a material asset in the project

summary.para: Descriptor defines a potential problem and a recommended course of action.
Affected areas

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.Descriptor.#ctor(System.String,System.String,Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.Areas,System.String,System.String)

summary.para: Initializes and returns an instance of Descriptor.

member.param: The Issue ID string.
A short human-readable 'name' for the issue
The area(s) affected by this issue.
A description of the issue.
Advice on how to resolve the issue.

summary.para: Default Severity of the issue.
Description of the issue.
URL to documentation.

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.Descriptor.Equals(Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.Descriptor)

summary.para: Returns true if the Descriptor is equal to a given Descriptor, false otherwise.

member.param: The Descriptor to compare equality with.

returns.para: The result of the equality comparison.

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.Descriptor.Equals(System.Object)

summary.para: Returns true if the Descriptor is equal to a given object, false otherwise.

member.param: The object to compare equality with.

returns.para: The result of the equality comparison.

summary.para: Optional Auto-Fixer
Returns the hash code for the Descriptor's Issue ID.

returns.para: The computed hash code.

summary.para: An unique identifier for the issue. IDs must have exactly 3 upper case characters, followed by 4 digits.
Returns true if the issue is enabled by default.
Maximum Unity version this issue applies to. If not specified, the issue applies to all versions.
Message used to describe a specific instance of the issue.
Name of the method of a known code API issue.
Minimum Unity version this issue applies to. If not specified, the issue applies to all versions.
Affected platforms. If null, the issue applies to all platforms.
Recommendation to fix the issue.
Return type of a known code API issue. See https:jira.unity3d.combrowsePROFB-318 for more details on why this is useful.
Issue title
Name of the type (namespace and class/struct) of a known code API issue.
The evaluated value of a know code API issue.
An unique identifier for an Issue Descriptor. IDs must have exactly 3 upper case characters, followed by 4 digits.
Get the hashed integer representation of the ID

returns.para: The ID as an int

summary.para: Get the string representation of the ID

returns.para: The ID as a string

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.DescriptorId.#ctor(System.String)

summary.para: Constructor

member.param: String representing a valid and unique Descriptor ID. IDs must have exactly 3 upper case characters, followed by 4 digits.

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.DescriptorId.Equals(Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.DescriptorId)

summary.para: Checks whether two DescriptorIDs contain the same ID.

member.param: A DescriptorId to compare to.

returns.para: True if the descriptors are the same, false if they are different.

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.DescriptorId.Equals(System.String)

summary.para: Checks whether two DescriptorIDs contain the same ID.

member.param: A string representing a descriptor ID to compare to.

returns.para: True if the descriptors are the same, false if they are different.

summary.para: Get the Descriptor which corresponds to this ID.

returns.para: The Descriptor which corresponds to this ID. If the ID is invalid, throws an exception.

summary.para: Implicit conversion of string to DescriptorId.

member.param: A string to convert

returns.para: A DescriptorId constructed using the string ID

returns.para: The integer hash of the ID

member.param: A DescriptorId to convert

returns.para: A string representation of the ID

summary.para: Checks whether the ID has been successfully constructed from a correctly-formatted string.

returns.para: True if the ID is valid

summary.para: Unity calls this method automatically after deserialization.
Unity calls this method automatically before serialization.
Returns the string representation of the ID

returns.para: The DescriptorId in string form.

summary.para: Provides numeric parameters with arbitrary names and defaults, with optional overrides for individual target build platforms.

summary.para: Copy constructor

member.param: The DiagnosticParams object to copy from.

summary.para: Draw project settings GUI for a specific BuildTargetGroup.

member.param: The BuildTargetGroup to draw the project settings UI for.

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.DiagnosticParams.GetDefault(System.String)

summary.para: Get the default value for this parameter.

member.param: Parameter key to look up.

returns.para: The default value for the key if it can be found, or 0 if not.

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.DiagnosticParams.GetParameter(System.String)

summary.para: Get the value of a named parameter. The parameter must have previously been registered with the RegisterParameter method.

member.param: Parameter name to look up.

returns.para: The parameter value for the currently-set analysis platform.

summary.para: Unity calls this method automatically after deserialization.
Unity calls this method automatically before serialization.

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.DiagnosticParams.RegisterParameter(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Int32)

summary.para: Register a parameter by declaring its name and default value. Parameters are registered on the "default" platform, so are available for retrieval on every target platform.

member.param: Parameter name.
A user friendly name to show in the Project Settings.
Text to show on a tooltip in the Project Settings.
The default value this parameter will have, unless it has already been registered.

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.DiagnosticParams.RegisterParameter(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)

summary.para: Register a parameter by declaring its name and default value. Parameters are registered on the "default" platform, so are available for retrieval on every target platform.

member.param: Parameter name.
A user friendly name to show in the Project Settings.
Text to show on a tooltip in the Project Settings.
The default value this parameter will have, unless it has already been registered.
The minimum valid value this parameter may have.
The maximum valid value this parameter may have.

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.DiagnosticParams.SetAnalysisPlatform(UnityEditor.BuildTarget)

summary.para: Sets the target analysis platform. When retrieving parameters, DiagnosticParams will first check the values specific to this platform.

member.param: Target platform for analysis.

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.DiagnosticParams.SetParameter(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Int32,UnityEditor.BuildTargetGroup)

summary.para: Set the value of a named parameter for a given analysis platform.

member.param: Parameter name to set.
A user friendly name to show in project settings.
Text to show on a tooltip in project settings.
Value to set the parameter to.
Analysis target platform for which to set the value. Defaults to BuildTarget.NoTarget which sets the value for the default platform.

summary.para: Provides methods to create and manage an object which can represent progress of the project analysis process.

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.IProgress.Advance(System.String)

summary.para: Advances the progress object by one step.

member.param: Updated message

summary.para: Clear and hide the progress object.
Checks if the progress operation has been cancelled.

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.IProgress.Start(System.String,System.String,System.Int32)

summary.para: Initializes the progress object.

member.param: Title
Description
Number of steps

summary.para: Identifiers for all the categories of issues reported by Project Auditor.
Issues relating to animation clips
Issues relating to animator controllers
Category for information about all of the code assemblies in the project
Issues relating to asset data or asset import settings
Issues relating to Audio Clip assets and import settings
Issues relating to avatars
Issues relating to avatar masks
Category for displaying information about files created during the project build process
Category for displaying information about the steps of the build process and how long they took
Category for build summary information
Code Issues, discovered by static code analysis
Compiler errors and warnings generated whilst compiling code for static analysis
Category for displaying variants of compute shaders
Issues that could result in undesired behavior if domain reloading is disabled
Enum value indicating the first available custom category
Category for showing materials grouped by shader
Issues relating to Mesh assets and import settings
Category for General statistics about the analysis process and its results.
Category for displaying installed packages
Category for information about precompiled assemblies
Issues relating to project settings
Category for reporting shaders in the project
Issues reported by the shader compiler
Category for reporting shader variants
Issues relating to Sprite Atlas assets and import settings
Issues relating to texture assets and texture import settings
Represents the location of a reported issue.
Constructor

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.Location.#ctor(System.String)

summary.para: Constructor

member.param: File path

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.Location.#ctor(System.String,System.Int32)

summary.para: Constructor with line number

member.param: File path
Line number

summary.para: File extension
Filename
Formatted filename with line number
Formatted path with line number
Checks whether the location is valid
Line number
Post-serialize callback.
Pre-serialize callback.
Full path
Describes the message log level of a code issue
An error message
A general information message
A warning message
The ProjectAuditor class is responsible for auditing the Unity project.
Returns the relative callback order for callbacks. Callbacks with lower values are called before ones with higher values.

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.ProjectAuditor.Audit(Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.IProgress)

summary.para: Performs static analysis of the project, using default parameters.

member.param: Progress bar, if applicable

returns.para: Generated report

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.ProjectAuditor.Audit(Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.AnalysisParams,Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.IProgress)

summary.para: Performs static analysis of the project, using the supplied analysis parameters.

member.param: Parameters to control the audit process
Progress bar, if applicable

returns.para: Generated report

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.ProjectAuditor.AuditAsync(Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.AnalysisParams,Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.IProgress)

summary.para: Performs asynchronous static analysis of the project, using the supplied analysis parameters. Provide a callback to the OnCompleted Action in analysisParams to obtain the Report when analysis is completed.

member.param: Parameters to control the audit process
Progress bar, if applicable

summary.para: ProjectAuditor default constructor

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.ProjectAuditor.OnPostprocessBuild(UnityEditor.Build.Reporting.BuildReport)

summary.para: Callback function which is called after a build is completed. If UserPreferences.AnalyzeAfterBuild is true, performs a full audit and logs the number of issues found.

member.param: A report containing information about the build, such as its target platform and output path.

summary.para: Report contains a list of all issues found by ProjectAuditor.
A name to display along with the Report, configurable by the user.

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.Report.FindByCategory(Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.IssueCategory)

summary.para: find all issues for a specific IssueCategory.

member.param: Desired IssueCategory

returns.para: Array of project issues

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.Report.FindByDescriptorId(System.String)

summary.para: Find all Issues that match a specific ID.

member.param: Desired Descriptor ID

returns.para: Array of project issues

summary.para: Gets a read-only collection of all of the ProjectIssues included in the report.

returns.para: All the issues in the report

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.Report.GetNumIssues(Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.IssueCategory)

summary.para: Get total number of issues for a specific IssueCategory.

member.param: Desired IssueCategory

returns.para: Number of project issues

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.Report.HasCategory(Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.IssueCategory)

summary.para: Checks whether the Report includes analysis for a given IssueCategory.

member.param: The IssuesCategory to check

returns.para: True if ProjectAuditor ran one or more Modules that reports issues of the specified IssueCategory. Otherwise, returns false.

summary.para: Check whether all issues in the report are valid.

returns.para: True is none of the issues in the report have a null description string. Otherwise returns false.

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.Report.Load(System.String,System.String&)

summary.para: Load a Report from a JSON file at the specified path.

member.param: File path of the report to load
Error message if load fails

returns.para: A loaded Report object

summary.para: The total number of ProjectIssues included in this report.
File format version of the Report (read-only).

member.name: M:Unity.ProjectAuditor.Editor.Report.Save(System.String)

summary.para: Save the Report as a JSON file.

member.param: The file path at which to save the file

summary.para: Describes an issue that ProjectAuditor reports in the Unity project.
This issue's category (read-only).
Custom properties. See the "moduleMetadata" section of an exported Report JSON file for information on the formats and meanings of the custom properties for each IssueCategory.
Project issue Description (read-only).
File extension of the file that contains this issue.
Name of the file that contains this issue.
An unique identifier for the issue descriptor (read-only).
Checks whether this ReportItem represents an Issue.

returns.para: True if the issue's descriptor ID is valid. Otherwise, returns false.

summary.para: Checks whether this issue is valid.

returns.para: True if the issue has a valid description string. Otherwise, returns false.

summary.para: Line in the file that contains this issue.
Location of the Insight or Issue (read-only).
Log level.
Relative path of the file that contains this issue.
Issue-specific Severity (read-only).
Represents a rule which modifies the Severity of an Issue ReportItem or all of the ProjectIssues that share a Descriptor.

An optional location filter representing a ReportItem's location. If specified, this Rule applies to a single ReportItem. If the string is null or empty, this Rule applies to every ReportItem matching the Id.
Get the hashed integer representation of the Rule.

returns.para: The computed hash code.

summary.para: The Descriptor ID
The Severity level to apply to the issue(s) represented by this Rule
Severity of an issue
Critical impact on performance, quality or functionality
Default Severity
An error that will prevent a successful build - for example, a code compile error encountered during code analysis
Not visible to user
Something which is reported for informational purposes only, not necessarily a problem
Significant impact
Minor impact
Moderate impact
Suppressed, ignored by UI and build
A compiler warning encountered during code analysis
